title: Return of the Schema
abstract: . Datasets for the experimental evaluation of knowledge graph refinement algorithms typically contain only ground facts, retaining very limited schema level knowledge even when such information is available in the source knowledge graphs. This limits the evaluation of methods that rely on rich ontological constraints, reasoning or neurosymbolic tech- niques and ultimately prevents assessing their performance in large-scale, real-world knowledge graphs. In this paper, we present KG-SaF the first resource that provides a workflow for extracting datasets including both schema and ground facts, ready for machine learning and reasoning ser- vices, along with the resulting curated suite of datasets. The workflow also handles inconsistencies detected when keeping both schema and facts and also leverage reasoning for entailing implicit knowledge. The suite includes newly extracted datasets from KGs with expressive schemas while simultaneously enriching existing datasets with schema informa- tion. Each dataset is serialized in OWL making it ready for reasoning services. Moreover, we provide utilities for loading datasets in tensor representations typical of standard machine learning libraries. Keywords: Knowledge Graphs · Ontologies · Machine Learning · Link Prediction. Resource type: Dataset Suite License: MIT License DOI: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17817931 URL: https://github.com/ivandiliso/kg-saf Documentation: https://ivandiliso.github.io/kg-saf/
summary:
This research paper introduces KG-SaF, a novel resource for evaluating knowledge graph refinement algorithms. The current datasets used for this purpose typically only contain ground facts, limiting the assessment of methods that rely on rich ontological constraints, reasoning, or neurosymbolic techniques. KG-SaF provides a workflow for extracting datasets that include both schema and ground facts, making them suitable for machine learning and reasoning services.

The workflow handles inconsistencies and leverages reasoning to entail implicit knowledge. The resulting suite of datasets includes newly extracted datasets from knowledge graphs with expressive schemas, as well as existing datasets enriched with schema information. Each dataset is serialized in OWL, making it ready for reasoning services, and utilities are provided for loading datasets in tensor representations compatible with standard machine learning libraries.

The key features of KG-SaF are:

* A workflow for extracting datasets with both schema and ground facts
* Handling of inconsistencies and leveraging reasoning for implicit knowledge
* A suite of curated datasets, including newly extracted and enriched existing datasets
* Serialization in OWL for reasoning services
* Utilities for loading datasets in tensor representations for machine learning

The resource is available under the MIT License and can be accessed through the provided URL, DOI, and documentation. The KG-SaF resource aims to facilitate the evaluation of knowledge graph refinement algorithms in large-scale, real-world knowledge graphs, particularly those that rely on rich ontological constraints, reasoning, or neurosymbolic techniques.
